papers.title: MosaicMem: Hybrid Spatial Memory for Controllable Video World Models
papers.authors: Wei Yu, Runjia Qian, Yumeng Li, Liquan Wang, Songheng Yin, Sri Siddarth Chakaravarthy P, Dennis Anthony, Yang Ye, Yidi Li, Weiwei Wan, Animesh Garg
papers.abstract: Autoregressive interactive minute-long video generation with promptable world events, using a hybrid spatial memory architecture for controllable video world models.

papers.title: MosaicMem V2: Object-Centric Controllability on a Memory Canvas for AI Game Worlds
papers.authors: Wei Yu, Runjia Qian, Dennis Anthony, Yumeng Li, Weiwei Wan, Animesh Garg
papers.abstract: Object-centric controllable generation on a persistent memory canvas, targeting interactive AI game world creation.

papers.title: WristPlay: Reframing Bimanual Robotic Manipulation as Multiplayer Video Generation
papers.authors: Wei Yu, Liquan Wang, Runjia Qian, Yumeng Li, Weiwei Wan, Animesh Garg
papers.abstract: A new framework that reframes bimanual robotic manipulation tasks as a multiplayer video generation problem, enabling richer coordination and control.

papers.title: EgoSim: Egocentric Exploration in Virtual Worlds with Multi-modal Conditioning
papers.authors: Wei Yu, Songheng Yin, Steve Easterbrook, Animesh Garg
papers.abstract: A framework for egocentric exploration in virtual worlds, enabling agents to navigate and interact using multi-modal conditioning signals. Presented as an oral at the ICML 1st Workshop on Controllable Video Generation.

papers.title: Modular Action Concept Grounding in Semantic Video Prediction
papers.authors: Wei Yu, Wenxin Chen, Songheng Yin, Steve Easterbrook, Animesh Garg
papers.abstract: One of the earliest works to investigate compositional text-to-video generation. We propose grounding modular action concepts in a semantic video prediction framework, enabling structured, interpretable video synthesis driven by language.

papers.title: Efficient and Information-Preserving Future Frame Prediction and Beyond
papers.authors: Wei Yu, Yichao Lu, Steve Easterbrook, Sanja Fidler
papers.abstract: The first video generation model with spatial compression in the latent space. This work also discovered what we now call generative test-time training, demonstrating efficiency and strong generalization in future frame prediction.

papers.title: CrevNet: Conditionally Reversible Video Prediction
papers.authors: Wei Yu, Yichao Lu, Steve Easterbrook, Sanja Fidler
papers.abstract: A conditionally reversible architecture for video prediction. Achieved 3rd place in the Traffic4Cast Challenge at NeurIPS, demonstrating strong generalization to complex real-world spatiotemporal forecasting tasks.

papers.title: Application of Deep Learning to Estimate Atmospheric Gravity Wave Parameters in Reanalysis Data Sets
papers.authors: Daisuke Matsuoka, Shingo Watanabe, Kaoru Sato, Sho Kawazoe, Wei Yu, Steve Easterbrook
papers.abstract: Application of deep learning methods to estimate parameters of atmospheric gravity waves from reanalysis datasets, bridging machine learning and climate science.

papers.title: Recovering the Parameters Underlying the Lorenz-96 Chaotic Dynamics
papers.authors: Soukayna Mouatadid, Pierre Gentine, Wei Yu, Steve Easterbrook
papers.abstract: A machine learning approach to recovering governing parameters of the Lorenz-96 dynamical system, with implications for climate modeling and chaotic system identification. Presented at the ICML 2019 Workshop on Climate Change.

papers.title: See, Plan, Predict: Language-Guided Cognitive Planning with Video Prediction
papers.authors: Maria Attarian, Advaya Gupta, Ziyi Zhou, Wei Yu, Igor Gilitschenski, Animesh Garg
papers.abstract: A language-guided cognitive planning framework that leverages video prediction as a world model, enabling agents to reason about future states and execute plans from natural language instructions.
